bio.quote: Yrsa Frostbane leads the Pale Maw Strike Team that attacks Surt’s Forge in Beat 18, but only if Thráinn Ashward was killed in Beat 16. She is battle-worn and grief-hardened, carrying the weight of abs

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

novice.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

novice.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

system.description: If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a free action to end one condition.

novice.description: If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a free action to end one condition.

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

novice.description:

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

system.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Strong, Persuasive.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 50

Perk: *Take the Hit*. Once per encounter, when an ally nearby would suffer the consequences of a hazard or complication, you can step in to take it instead.

novice.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Strong, Persuasive.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 50

Perk: *Take the Hit*. Once per encounter, when an ally nearby would suffer the consequences of a hazard or complication, you can step in to take it instead.

system.description: Whenever you fail a Might test, you can lose Toughness equal to {1d6 + your level} to improve the outcome of the test by one tier. You can use this perk only once per test.

novice.description: Whenever you fail a Might test, you can lose Toughness equal to {1d6 + your level} to improve the outcome of the test by one tier. You can use this perk only once per test.

system.description:
Demons and deathless fear you. Criminals run from the sight of your shadow. Agents of chaos, blasphemers, and heretics tremble at the sound of your voice. You carry the power of the gods, armed with wrath and sent out into the world first to seek, then censor those whose actions—or even existence—are anathema to your church. 

As a censor, you’re at your best against the strongest foes. Your judgment terrifies heretics, stops enemies in their tracks, and even hurls them across the battlefield.

novice.description:
Demons and deathless fear you. Criminals run from the sight of your shadow. Agents of chaos, blasphemers, and heretics tremble at the sound of your voice. You carry the power of the gods, armed with wrath and sent out into the world first to seek, then censor those whose actions—or even existence—are anathema to your church. 

As a censor, you’re at your best against the strongest foes. Your judgment terrifies heretics, stops enemies in their tracks, and even hurls them across the battlefield.

system.description: Corruption has deep tendrils that can be missed, leading you to specialize in uncovering clandestine threats to your order. You have the Magic skill.

novice.description: Corruption has deep tendrils that can be missed, leading you to specialize in uncovering clandestine threats to your order. You have the Magic skill.

system.description: The first time on a turn that you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement} ability to judge a creature, you can deal to the judged creature.

novice.description: The first time on a turn that you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement} ability to judge a creature, you can deal to the judged creature.

system.description: Your order has trained you to understand fragments of the visions granted to you by your deity, giving you a momentary second chance (two rolls, take best) in challenging situations. At the start of an encounter, you can take one main action before any other creature and before your first turn. Addi tionally, whenever the Director calls for a montage test, you can make one free test before the montage begins, which counts as an earned success or failure as usual

novice.description: Your order has trained you to understand fragments of the visions granted to you by your deity, giving you a momentary second chance (two rolls, take best) in challenging situations. At the start of an encounter, you can take one main action before any other creature and before your first turn. Addi tionally, whenever the Director calls for a montage test, you can make one free test before the montage begins, which counts as an earned success or failure as usual

system.description: Your focus on your fragmentary visions grants divine insight into the world and its creatures beyond your usual senses. Whenever you would make an Intuition test, you can make a Presence test instead.

novice.description: Your focus on your fragmentary visions grants divine insight into the world and its creatures beyond your usual senses. Whenever you would make an Intuition test, you can make a Presence test instead.

system.description: Each time you finish a extended rest, you can choose yourself or one ally who is also finishing a extended rest to gain the benefit of a divine ritual. The chosen character gains a bonus to their recovery value equal to [[lookup @level]]{your level} that lasts until you finish another extended rest.

novice.description: Each time you finish a extended rest, you can choose yourself or one ally who is also finishing a extended rest to gain the benefit of a divine ritual. The chosen character gains a bonus to their recovery value equal to [[lookup @level]]{your level} that lasts until you finish another extended rest.

system.description: Your divine presence causes those you deem worthy to recover quickly from a fight. Whenever an ally within distance of your My Life for Yours ability regains Toughness, they regain additional {Toughness} equal to your Presence score.

novice.description: Your divine presence causes those you deem worthy to recover quickly from a fight. Whenever an ally within distance of your My Life for Yours ability regains Toughness, they regain additional {Toughness} equal to your Presence score.

system.description: The Guisarmier kit is for those who want to use a polearm for extended reach while remaining protected by sturdy armor. This is the kit that allows you to become the ultimate halberd, longspear, or glaive fighter

novice.description: The Guisarmier kit is for those who want to use a polearm for extended reach while remaining protected by sturdy armor. This is the kit that allows you to become the ultimate halberd, longspear, or glaive fighter

items.name: Look On My Work and Despair

system.description: Your judgment has grown in divine power, instilling fear in those you condemn. Whenever you use your Judgment ability, you can spend 1 wrath, and if the target has [[potency P average]], they are frightened of you (save ends). Additionally, whenever a creature judged by you is reduced to 0 Toughness and you use Judgment as a free triggered action, if the new target has [[potency P strong]], they are frightened of you (save ends). If the target is already frightened of you, they instead take {holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score.

novice.description: Your judgment has grown in divine power, instilling fear in those you condemn. Whenever you use your Judgment ability, you can spend 1 wrath, and if the target has [[potency P average]], they are frightened of you (save ends). Additionally, whenever a creature judged by you is reduced to 0 Toughness and you use Judgment as a free triggered action, if the new target has [[potency P strong]], they are frightened of you (save ends). If the target is already frightened of you, they instead take {holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score.

system.description: Whenever you make a test to read a person and obtain a tier 3 outcome, you notice several tells that give away their true feelings. Any test you make to read that person in the future gains an advantage (second chance).

novice.description: Whenever you make a test to read a person and obtain a tier 3 outcome, you notice several tells that give away their true feelings. Any test you make to read that person in the future gains an advantage (second chance).

system.description: On any day when you don’t take a extended rest, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a crafting project using a skill you have from the crafting skill group. If you do so, you gain project points toward that project.

novice.description: On any day when you don’t take a extended rest, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a crafting project using a skill you have from the crafting skill group. If you do so, you gain project points toward that project.

system.description:
The power you serve grants you a Heroic Resource called wrath, fueling your abilities as you censor those your church deems to be heretics.

Wrath in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain wrath equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you {gain 2 wrath}.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that a creature judged by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement}) deals damage to you, you {gain 1 wrath}. The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you {gain 1 wrath}.

You lose any remaining wrath at the end of the encounter.

Wrath Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain wrath outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost wrath without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs wrath, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited wrath on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of wrath equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. (Such abilities aren’t part of the core rules for the censor, but they might appear in future products.)

system.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you gain 2 wrath instead of 1.

novice.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you gain 2 wrath instead of 1.
